title: Delete Resource interface and shared code
description: Remove Resource interface, shared reconciliation abstractions, and any orphaned code after domain-specific implementations are working.

title: Implement hardcoded cargo manager
description: Create dedicated cargo manager. Self-installation via rustup. Handle cargo install idempotency.
close_reason: Implemented CargoManager with PackageManager/PackageUpgrader interfaces, rustup self-install, and idempotent error handling.

title: Update package manager tests
description: Update/rewrite tests for new hardcoded manager implementations. Remove tests for deleted config-driven functionality.

title: Add GemManager as built-in hardcoded manager
close_reason: GemManager implemented with full test coverage

title: Refactor dotfile reconciliation as standalone
description: Extract dotfile handling from Resource interface into its own domain. Keep existing state tracking, drift detection - just remove the shared abstraction layer.

title: Implement hardcoded npm-family manager
description: Create npm manager supporting provider swapping (npm/pnpm/bun). Single config option for which provider to use. Self-installation support for each provider.
close_reason: Implemented NPMManager with npm/pnpm/bun provider swapping, provider-specific commands, JSON parsing, and self-install support.

title: Simplify orchestrator to domain glue
description: Reduce orchestrator to: run package reconciliation, run dotfile reconciliation, report results. Remove Resource abstraction handling.

title: Audit Resource interface and reconciliation engine
description: Map current Resource interface, shared reconciliation code, orchestrator complexity. Identify what packages vs dotfiles actually need differently.

title: Implement hardcoded go manager
description: Create dedicated go manager for 'go install' packages. Self-installation strategy TBD (brew? official installer?).
close_reason: Implemented GoManager with GOBIN scanning for list, binary removal for uninstall, and brew-based self-install.

title: Remove config-driven package managers
description: Kill GenericManager and YAML-based manager configuration. Hardcode support for: brew, npm-family (with provider swapping for pnpm/bun), cargo, go, uv. Restore self-installation support with ability to configure bootstrap method per manager.

title: Remove shared Resource abstraction
description: Kill the unified Resource interface and shared reconciliation engine. Implement domain-specific reconciliation: packages get simple list-based reconciliation, dotfiles keep their own state tracking. Orchestrator becomes trivial glue between the two domains.

title: Update reconciliation tests
description: Update tests for new domain-specific reconciliation. Remove tests for deleted Resource abstraction.

title: Implement hardcoded uv manager
description: Create dedicated uv manager. Self-installation via brew or official script. Handle uv tool install semantics.
close_reason: Implemented UVManager with uv tool commands, upgrade-all support, and brew/script self-install.

title: Implement simple package reconciliation
description: Replace Resource-based package handling with simple list-based reconciliation: diff lock file specs against installed packages, install/remove as needed. No shared abstractions.

title: Remove GenericManager and YAML config code
description: Delete GenericManager, manager config loading, YAML manager definitions, ManagerRegistry complexity. Clean removal after hardcoded managers are working.
close_reason: Removed GenericManager, YAML config loading, and metadata extractors. Updated registry to use hardcoded switch, simplified helpers with hardcoded install hints, and fixed all tests.

title: Implement hardcoded brew manager
description: Create dedicated brew manager with: install/uninstall/list commands, self-installation via official script, idempotent error handling. No YAML config.
close_reason: Implemented BrewManager with all PackageManager and PackageUpgrader methods, idempotent error handling, and self-installation support. Added comprehensive tests.

title: Audit current GenericManager and config-driven manager code
description:
## Audit: GenericManager and Config-Driven Manager Code

### Files to Delete/Replace

**Core Implementation (DELETE)**
- `internal/resources/packages/generic.go` (386 lines) - GenericManager implementation
- `internal/resources/packages/generic_test.go` (680 lines) - tests

**Config Types (SIMPLIFY)**
- `internal/config/managers.go` (182 lines) - ManagerConfig, ListConfig, CommandConfig, etc.
- `internal/config/managers_defaults.go` (245 lines) - GetDefaultManagers() with 8 manager configs
- `internal/config/user_defined.go` (291 lines) - diff/merge logic for user overrides

**Registry (SIMPLIFY)**  
- `internal/resources/packages/registry.go` (107 lines) - v2Managers map, LoadV2Configs

### Current Default Managers (in managers_defaults.go)
- pipx ❌ REMOVE
- cargo ✅ KEEP  
- gem ❌ REMOVE
- brew ✅ KEEP
- npm ✅ KEEP (with provider swapping)
- pnpm ✅ KEEP (as npm provider)
- conda ❌ REMOVE
- uv ✅ KEEP

### Missing Manager
- go ⚠️ ADD NEW - not currently implemented

### Impact Radius (files using GenericManager/ManagerRegistry)
Commands: install.go, uninstall.go, upgrade.go
Clone: setup.go
Diagnostics: health.go
Resources: operations.go, spec.go, resource.go, init.go
~15 test files

### Tests to Update (~1563 lines total)
- generic_test.go (680 lines) - DELETE entirely
- registry_test.go (48 lines) - heavy refactor
- registry_v2_test.go (146 lines) - DELETE entirely  
- managers_test.go (201 lines) - simplify
- user_defined_test.go (317 lines) - DELETE entirely
- validators_test.go (171 lines) - simplify (remove listconfig validator)

### Key Decisions for Implementation
1. Create dedicated manager files: brew.go, npm.go, cargo.go, go.go, uv.go
2. Each implements PackageManager interface directly
3. npm.go handles provider swapping (npm/pnpm/bun)
4. Remove all YAML config parsing for managers
5. Registry becomes simple name→manager lookup
6. Self-installation support per manager
close_reason: Audit complete. Documented all files to delete/modify, impact radius, and implementation decisions.
